steps.title: Read the y-intercept
steps.content: The line crosses the $y$-axis at $12$, so the equation has $b=12$ in slope-intercept form.

steps.title: Find the slope
steps.content:
Use two clear points on the line, such as $(0,12)$ and $(6,6)$. Then
$$m=\frac{6-12}{6-0}=\frac{-6}{6}=-1$$

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content:
With slope $-1$ and y-intercept $12$, the equation is
$$y=-x+12$$
The answer is B.
